claims.claim: Conor McGregor is most effective as a fighter when he is moving forward and dictating the pace.
claims.quote: He's a forward fighting fighter. And so the biggest key for Max is to get him moving back. But again, I want to point out that this is southpaw versus southpaw.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor struggles to land his shots and becomes uncomfortable when he is forced to fight off his back foot.
claims.quote: When Connor's backed up, he is uncomfortable and so he can't land his same shots backing up.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's ability to control range and counter is significantly diminished against other southpaws because he cannot utilize his preferred lead-hand fighting.
claims.quote: And against Dustin Porier, he didn't have that because he doesn't have the same range that he's used to having because the hands are on opposite side.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor does not spend much time training off his back and is not seasoned in ground fighting from that position.
claims.quote: He probably doesn't get put there very much in practice. When I watch him, he's not on his back very much in practice. He doesn't spend a lot of time there.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has more experience and success fighting conventional (right-handed) fighters than he does fighting other southpaws.
claims.quote: So he has more reps on that track than Max has against a southpaw. So basically, Max is doing the track backwards and Connor is doing it the way he always does.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's performance in his first fight against Max Holloway represents the best version of him as a fighter.
claims.quote: I think that's why this fight is sparking so much controversy, so much talk, is because this version of Connor is the best Connor you'll ever see.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor struggles when forced to move backward.
claims.quote: if you get Conor McGregor moving back, he doesn't have a lot of answers. He's not good there.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor's fighting style relies on forward-fainting pressure to build momentum.
claims.quote: all of his momentum is not off the back foot. It's built off of his forward fainting pressure.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor is more effective against conventional right-handed fighters than southpaws.
claims.quote: This is what Connor does better than anybody against a right-handed fighter.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor uses hand fighting to neutralize the lead hand of conventional fighters.
claims.quote: he takes away one of your weapons, which is that lead hand by hand fighting.

claims.claim: Max Holloway can neutralize Conor McGregor's counters by keeping him moving backward.
claims.quote: If you can continually keep Connor moving back you have a chance.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has been using performance-enhancing drugs during his five-year layoff.
claims.quote: A version of Connor that's been out 5 years on performance-enhancing drugs. Who is that guy? We have no idea.

claims.claim: Moving the fight to 170 lbs puts Max Holloway at a disadvantage compared to his natural weight class.
claims.quote: fighting him at a at max's true weight class where we know we would see the best Max.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor is currently competing at 170 lbs.
claims.quote: he's at 170 lb.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor will perform better in his return than he did in the previous five years.
claims.quote: I think we're going to get a better one personally

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has used performance-enhancing drugs during his recovery.
claims.quote: he's admitted that that has been used on the outside to get to where he's at now.

claims.claim: Max Holloway does not have any professional fights at 170 lbs.
claims.quote: Max doesn't have one fight at 170.

claims.claim: Max Holloway will not be at his best performance level if he fights at 170 lbs.
claims.quote: it doesn't mean we're going to get the best version of Max at that weight.

claims.claim: Max Holloway walks around at approximately 185 to 190 lbs.
claims.quote: he probably walks around at 185 lbs, maybe 190.

claims.claim: Max Holloway's speed, fluidity, and athleticism will be negatively impacted by moving up to 170 lbs.
claims.quote: When you make this fight at 170, you take away Max's speed. You take away all his best advantages.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor retains his power advantage at 170 lbs.
claims.quote: the gift that Connor has is his power. He gets to keep that gift. At 170, Max only loses gifts at 170.

claims.claim: Max Holloway is an accumulative puncher who relies on volume and combinations rather than one-punch power.
claims.quote: He's an accumulative puncher. He throws punches and bunches. He throws combinations.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor is a counter-fighter who relies on rhythm and timing.
claims.quote: Connor is a counter fighter with rhythm and timing, but he's got big power.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor has used performance-enhancing drugs during his recovery from injury.
claims.quote: it doesn't take away from the fact that he's admitted that that has been used on the outside to get to where he's at now.

claims.claim: Conor McGregor is coming off a five-year layoff.
claims.quote: A five-year layoff with Connor peeds. You don't know.
